summary: Backend (13 pytest cases) and Frontend (Playwright) e2e testing completed for Clan War Tracker. All critical flows pass: auth (login/wrong creds/me/protected endpoints), folder CRUD with default-protection, clan CRUD with Royale API integration, manual & global sync, history with date filter, and full UI navigation (login → overview → clan detail → history → logout). Player links correctly open RoyaleAPI in new tab. Test clan #2PP works end-to-end, #LR0CV8YV returns 404 on currentriverrace so its sync is marked failed (Royale-API-side, not a bug, but backend could downgrade to a softer state).

minor.issue: Propagates Royale API 404 as HTTP 404 to client when clan has no current river race. Consider catching and returning a notInWar snapshot instead of failing. Currently auto-sync also counts it as a hard failure (e.g. XD clan continually shows '1 hata').
normalize_tag replaces every 'O' with '0' which is too aggressive — many valid tags contain letter O. Could falsely reject/mismatch tags.
If initial sync fails, clan is still created without a snapshot; user sees 'henüz senkronize edilmedi'. Acceptable but error reason isn't surfaced in the response.

issues: '2 ok / 1 hata' shown but not clickable to view error details for the failing clan
Trash icons are opacity-0 by default and only show on hover; not very discoverable on touch devices. Acceptable on desktop.

action_items: (Optional, minor) Handle Royale API 404 inside sync_clan_data by saving a notInWar/empty snapshot instead of raising HTTPException so 'failed' count and UI badge stay clean for clans without an active river race.
(Optional) Reconsider normalize_tag replacing 'O' with '0' — Royale tags don't actually contain the letter O (Supercell omits it), so the rule is safe but could be made explicit/documented.
(Optional) Surface last_sync_error on clan cards (tooltip) so users understand why a clan shows '—' state.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py mixes normalize_tag(replace 'O' -> '0') which is correct for Supercell tags but should be documented as intentional.
Lifespan ensures default folder ('Genel') is created once, scheduler starts with configurable interval — clean.
CORS allow_credentials=True with allow_origins='*' — fine here since auth is Bearer in header, not cookies, but worth noting.
war_snapshots upsert keyed by (clan_id, snapshot_date) — overwrites within the day, which matches the daily history requirement.
delete_clan removes snapshots — good. Foreign key cleanup is consistent.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (login, overview, sidebar, add-folder, add-clan dialog open, clan-detail, history, player link, logout)

test_credentials: TK / 2903 (from backend/.env AUTH_USERNAME/AUTH_PASSWORD). No /app/memory/test_credentials.md file exists.
seed_data_creation: Test created and cleaned up: folder 'TEST_UI_Folder' and clan '#2PP' (re-added then deleted by lifecycle test, then re-added manually for UI test). Orphan clan #LR0CV8YV from a prior failed test iteration was also cleaned up. Final DB state: 2 clans (#2PP RoYaLe PiNoY, #GYUYCY9C Alper CR) + default 'Genel' folder.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All endpoints in /api are working with Bearer auth (jwt). Reliable test clan: #2PP. Avoid using #LR0CV8YV — currentriverrace returns 404 for it. Pytest file: /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py. data-testid coverage on UI is excellent.
rca of the issue: Initial pytest used #LR0CV8YV which returned 404 from Royale /currentriverrace. Manual sync surfaced the upstream 404 as a backend 404. Switching to #2PP resolved; underlying minor backend issue documented above.
